etymology.description: Original simplified form 从 -> semantic 辵(辵) (chuò, "walk; movement") + phonetic 从 (cóng, "follow") -> final form 從
etymology.interpretation: The character 從 is a phono-semantic compound, where the semantic element 辵(辵) (chuò, "walk; movement") indicates the action of moving or following, while the phonetic element 从 (cóng, "follow") suggests the pronunciation "cóng." The evolution from the simplified form to the traditional involved incorporating the semantic component to emphasize its meaning.
etymology.simplification: 從(從) was simplified to 从 by removing the 辵(辵) (chuò, "walk; movement") component, leaving the phonetic 从 (cóng, "follow") form.

examples.chinese: 我们从小就是好朋友(我們從小就是好朋友)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen cóng xiǎo jiù shì hǎo péngyǒu.
examples.english: We have been good friends since childhood.

examples.chinese: 小猫跟着母亲从灌木丛中走出来(小貓跟著母親從灌木叢中走出來)。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎo māo gēnzhe mǔqīn cóng guànmùcóng zhōng zǒuchūlái.
examples.english: The kitten followed its mother out of the shrubbery.

examples.chinese: 从南方看，风景十分壮丽(從南方看，風景十分壯麗)。
examples.pinyin: Cóng nánfāng kàn, fēngjǐng shífēn zhuànglì.
examples.english: The scenery is magnificent when viewed from the south.
